design_philosophy.core_loop: Daily cycle structure: shooting practice → action stages → quick draw duel. Player controls reticle across third-person and first-person perspective shifts. Cash earned from shooting enemies, spent to save progress or repair town damage.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management tension between accuracy (avoiding town damage/wasting bullets) and progression (paying to save). Secondary tension in final duel segments requiring precise timing.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as an anthropomorphic robot sheriff defending a frontier town against the Bad Oil Gang through shooting and quick-draw duels. Control flexibility (standard controller, Super Scope, SNES Mouse) reinforces player agency.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Penalty-based progression system where player performance directly impacts ending. Morality constraints (Good Guy Code of the West) force creative solutions. Financial consequences for missed shots and collateral damage. Multiple endings based on final cash balance ($0-750k/750k-999k/1M+) tie mechanical performance to narrative outcome.

difficulty.continues_system: Progress saved via cash payment; financial cost acts as continue penalty
difficulty.checkpointing: Daily checkpoints with cash-based save system. Loss of cash for poor shooting performance (wasted bullets, town damage) creates soft-failure state.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High. Player controls input method (controller/Super Scope/Mouse) affecting aim precision. Financial penalties are adjustable based on playstyle—aggressive shooting costs more but completes stages faster. Choice to hit/avoid canteens affects bonus stage availability.

progression.upgrade_system: Cash accumulation affects ending branches but not mechanical upgrades. Canteen pickups restore hit points as consumable recovery.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Seven-day linear structure (Monday-Sunday) with scripted story beats. 31 levels total across third-person and first-person perspectives, alternating with quick-draw duels.
replayability_hooks: Multiple endings tied to final cash balance (3 variants)
Control method choice affects difficulty and playstyle (controller vs Super Scope vs Mouse)
Bonus stage access conditional on avoiding canteens in main stages
Moral choice loopholes (shooting disguised women-and-children) enable different combat solutions

world_building_techniques: Character roster with distinct mechanical personalities (Snake Oil fast but inaccurate, Tiny Johnson slow but accurate)
Environmental storytelling through stage locations (saloon shootout, train robbery, bank heist, jail break, cattle stampede)
Dialogue-driven plot reveals (Good Guy Code of the West creates narrative constraints on gameplay)
Twist ending mechanic: Maria revealed as Black Bart in disguise if player earns 1M+ dollars
Economic narrative: town damage has cash consequences, tying player actions to world state

boss_design.philosophy: Quick-draw duel finale against each Bad Oil Gang member, thematically matching their stated weaknesses. Black Bart faces two encounters: moral/reputational defeat mid-story, final gunfight at climax. Kid Johnson presents moral constraint (cannot be shot as child) forcing non-violence resolution.
notable_bosses: Snake Oil (fast draw, poor accuracy—demonstrates speed vs precision trade-off)
Tiny Johnson (slow draw, perfect accuracy—mirror of Snake Oil)
The Kid Johnson (undefeatable due to Code of the West moral constraint)
Black Bart (dual encounters: narrative confrontation and final gunfight; can be defeated by proving Kid alive)

faction_indicators: Bad Oil Gang: criminal syndicate with clear hierarchy (Black Bart leader, Johnson brothers, Oil brothers)
East Driftwood townspeople: operate under 'Good Guy Code of the West' moral framework
Mo Crash sidekick and Schemp antagonist represent internal betrayal/loyalty dynamics
danger_signals: Bad Oil Gang disguise as women/children to exploit Code of the West
Prison escape and continued escalation from saloon shooting to bank robbery to train hijacking
Leader Black Bart's manipulation: hiring civilian to spread false accusations, framing Tin Star for child murder
Democratic justice subversion: Tin Star falsely convicted and badge revoked despite evidence
environmental_storytelling: Newly built railroad symbolizes town progress and becomes contested infrastructure
Town damage accumulation (cash penalty) reflects physical destruction from gang activity
Location progression (coach → saloon → jail → bank → desert) traces gang's escalating control
Headquarters destruction marks gang's territorial defeat

sources_used: tcrf: Tin Star (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
